summary: Stage the corrected complete M0-M6 application as Windows candidate 0.1.0-beta.3 without changing the still-public 0.1.0-beta.2 site, installer, or support claims before release proof exists.

providedInputs.label: Owner-provided ScadMill functional specification v0.6 with amendments through A-11

decisions.decision: Stage the A-11, first-run-copy, and release-enforcement corrections as 0.1.0-beta.3 while preserving the immutable beta.2 tag and artifact.
decisions.rationale: The existing beta.2 bytes cannot be changed in place. A new prerelease version is required to bind the corrected source, evidence, signature, and installer hash honestly.

decisions.decision: Advance CANDIDATE_VERSION and application manifests to beta.3 while retaining PUBLIC_VERSION, website release metadata, and supported-download documentation at beta.2 until the signed beta.3 installer passes and is published.
decisions.rationale: The signed installer hash and size do not exist before the hosted artifact is produced. Separating candidate and public facts prevents circular builds and false public download claims.

testEvidence.criterion: The candidate application may advance to beta.3 without mutating the current beta.2 public release contract.
testEvidence.test: Require application manifests, both Cargo lockfiles, application title, and CANDIDATE_VERSION to agree on beta.3 while PUBLIC_VERSION and website release.json continue identifying the exact beta.2 public artifact.

red.outcome: RED: the candidate contract expected 0.1.0-beta.3 but CANDIDATE_VERSION and application manifests still identified 0.1.0-beta.2. One test failed and three passed.

green.outcome: GREEN: all four release-metadata tests passed with beta.3 candidate metadata and the exact beta.2 public artifact contract unchanged.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
